node.statement: R well-defined with ||R-I|| <= C eta. Put U:=S^2-I, so ||U|| <= 4 eta < 1 for eta <= eta_0 < 1/4. Define R:=(S^2)^(-1/2)=(I+U)^(-1/2)=sum_{k>=0} C(-1/2,k) U^k. By GT-binomial-series this converges absolutely (sum_k |C(-1/2,k)| ||U||^k=(1-||U||)^(-1/2)<inf) in the complete order-unit space, as in the function calculus of GT-kitaev-spectral. Hence ||R-I|| <= sum_{k>=1}|C(-1/2,k)| ||U||^k=(1-||U||)^(-1/2)-1 <= C||U|| <= 4C eta for ||U||<=1/2, i.e. ||R-I|| <= C eta.
